sources.title: Beijing calls for de-escalation as Hormuz closure bites Chinese shippers
sources.summary: Fresh fixture URL adding the Chinese-industry perspective on Hormuz.

actors_quoted.name: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the People's Republic of China
actors_quoted.position: Calls for all parties to prioritise de-escalation and resume dialogue.

preliminary_divergences: English-language coverage foregrounds the military and diplomatic angles, while French sources emphasise casualties among European peacekeepers.
Western outlets frame the Strait of Hormuz closure as coercive blackmail, while Iranian state media frames it as a legitimate defensive response.
Chinese and South Asian coverage centres the economic downstream impact (shipping costs, fuel prices) rather than the bilateral US-Iran dimension.
coverage_gaps: No direct reporting from Gulf Arab states despite their exposure to a sustained Hormuz closure.
Limited perspective from the international shipping industry on insurance and route-diversion costs.
Absence of humanitarian reporting on civilian conditions inside Iran beyond aggregated casualty figures.
